person.userCertifiedLifeSummary: WALTER DE LINDSAY, was Lord of Lamberton before circa 1200, when he had licence from the Prior of Coldingham to have a private chapel in his castle there. He was Sheriff of Berwickshire, Constable of Berwick, and one of the Justiciars of Scotland. He held the manors of Fordington and Ulseby, co. Lincoln; and Molesworth, in the earldom of Huntingdon. He granted the churches of Fordington and Ulseby to the Abbey of Croyland, and lands to the Knight Templars. He was an ambassador to England 1215 to ask restitution of the earldom of Huntingdon, he witnessed many charters of Kings William and Alexander II, and died about 1221. His wife's name is unknown, but after his death she was married against her will, about 1222, to P. de Valence, when a dispensation was obtained for consanguinity.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter de Lindsay於大約1129年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾時，他的父親Sir William de Lindsay19歲，母親Alice Montgomery of Lancaster11歲。他和Mathilde De Penthievre至少有1個兒子。他去世於大約1168年在美國賓夕法尼亞州切斯特易亞斯特 法洛雷費爾德 堂史普艾希爾度享年41歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭藍納可爾克勞福德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lindsay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Lindsey in Lincolnshire, England. This is first found in the form Lindissi, apparently a derivative of the British name of Lincoln . To this was later added the Old English element ēg ‘island’, since the place was virtually cut off by the surrounding fenland. The surname was taken to Scotland at an early date and is the name of an important and powerful Scottish family.
Irish: adopted as an equivalent ofvarious Gaelic names (see   Lindsey  ).

variants.name: Linzy
meanings: Altered form of Scottish, Irish, or English   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  . Compare   Lenzy  .

variants.name: Linzey
meanings: English: variant of   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  .

variants.name: Lindsey
meanings: Irish: shortened and Anglicized form of various Gaelic surnames, as for example Ó Loingsigh (see Lynch 1), Mac Giolla Fhionntóg (see McClintock ), and Ó Fhloinn (see Flynn and Lynn ).

variants.name: Lincoln
meanings: English: habitational name from the city of Lincoln, so named from an original British name Lindo- ‘lake’ + Latin colonia ‘settlement, colony’. The place was an important administrative center during the Roman occupation of Britain and in the Middle Ages it was a center for the manufacture of cloth, including the famous ‘Lincoln green’.
African American: adoption of the surname in 1 above, in honor of Abraham Lincoln, the 16th president of the US, who abolished slavery.
History: Abraham Lincoln (1809–65), 16th president of the US, was the son of an illiterate laborer, descended from Samuel Lincoln, who had emigrated from England to MA in 1637.

variants.name: Linsey
meanings: Scottish, Irish, or English: variant of   Lindsay   or   Lindsey  .
Americanized form of German Linse or Swiss German Linsi, see Lins .

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Walter de Lindsay was born about 1129, in Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir William de Lindsay, was 19 and his mother, Alice Montgomery of Lancaster, was 11. He had at least 1 son with Mathilde De Penthievre. He died about 1168, in Ercildoun, East Fallowfield Township, Chester, Pennsylvania, United States, at the age of 41, and was buried in Crawford, Lanarkshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter de Lindsay родился около 1129 г. в местности линколншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir William de Lindsay было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Montgomery of Lancaster, 11. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mathilde De Penthievre. Он умер около 1168 г. в местности эрсаилдоун, эаст фаллоуфиелд тоуншип, честир, Пенсильвания, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 41 год и был похоронен в местности кравфорд, ланаркшир, Шотландия.
